element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns pole-grabbing rotation mechanic in initial stages
Simple uncovered ingots in early stages; later stages require multiple passes to toggle visibility
Essential navigation mechanic introduced immediately for maze traversal
Avoidance and stun mechanics introduced; later player learns to push stunned enemies into walls for points
Offensive mechanic to temporarily disable Unira threats
Instant death hazards requiring spatial awareness and careful route planning
Advanced puzzle mechanic requiring players to track traversal count and plan routes accordingly

element_introduction_order.first_appears: VS. Clu Clu Land arcade/FDS expanded edition
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Escalated enemy threat in expanded content

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully uncovering all ingots to reveal the puzzle shape
Pushing stunned Unira into walls for point rewards
Navigating safely past Black Holes without collision
typical_tension_triggers: Time running out (implicit life loss condition)
Proximity to Black Holes during maze navigation
Complexity of multi-pass ingot logic in later stages
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based puzzle pacing with escalating spatial complexity and enemy pressure; early stages emphasize learning controls, mid-to-late stages introduce simultaneous threat avoidance and puzzle logic

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial phase introducing Bubbles, ingots, turning posts, and basic Unira avoidance in controlled maze layouts
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased Unira frequency and Black Hole placement; introduction of Sound Wave stun mechanic for offensive play; maze complexity grows
difficulty_curve.late_game: Multi-pass ingot toggling mechanic creates puzzle logic layer on top of spatial navigation; requires route planning and mental tracking; simultaneous threat avoidance
notable_spikes: Introduction of Black Holes (instant death hazard)
Multi-pass ingot mechanic requiring odd-pass completion
VS. Clu Clu Land arcade version with Boss Unira and expanded level count

notable_level_patterns.description: Each stage layout contains golden ingots positioned to form a recognizable shape (heart, mushroom) when all are uncovered. Player must navigate the maze collecting all ingots to complete the visual reveal.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All levels follow this core pattern

notable_level_patterns.description: Maze structure designed around pole grabbing mechanic where Bubbles rotates around turning posts to change direction. Mazes constrain movement to discrete rotational paths rather than free movement.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All levels follow this core pattern

notable_level_patterns.description: Unira placement in later stages requires players to simultaneously avoid contact, use sound waves to stun when strategic, and push stunned enemies into walls for score gain without compromising puzzle progress.
Late-stage levels introduce ingots requiring odd number of passes to remain uncovered. Players must mentally track traversal count and plan routes to cross each ingot the correct parity of times.

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Clu Clu Land (NES)
